def.normie: A short video format that cuts from 'sorry I can't today, I have therapy' to whatever the creator actually does to feel better — a walk, a swim, baking, calling a friend. Wholesome and a little self-deprecating.
def.normal: Mid-June Reels format that hard-cuts from the therapy excuse to the creator's actual coping ritual. Works because the punchline is sincere; whatever you reveal lands as both a flex and a confession.
def.zoomer: Bro said 'I have therapy' and the next shot is him at Costco buying a single rotisserie chicken. The format is a vibe check disguised as a brag.

trends.name: Homer Drops His Donut (AI song)

def.normie: An AI-generated song where Homer Simpson 'sings' his 'D'oh!' over every minor inconvenience. People are using it for any moment where something small goes wrong.
def.normal: AI-slop-coded but somehow charming: the 'Homer Drops His Donut' track has become the universal small-disaster audio. The discourse about whether it's allowed to be funny is keeping it alive.
def.zoomer: AI Homer dropping the donut hits every micro-defeat in the timeline. It's slop. It's also undefeated. We will pretend we hate it for one more week.

trends.vid: homer drops his donut ai song

trends.name: Red vs. Blue Button dilemma

def.normie: A viral moral-dilemma post: 'Press the red button and X happens; press the blue button and Y happens.' People debate which they'd pick.
def.normal: The 'two-button moral dilemma' format went mainstream this week — every niche now has its own red-vs-blue version. The debates outlive the original prompts.
def.zoomer: Pick red (everyone dies but rent is free), pick blue (rent stays but you have to do small talk). It's just trolley problem but the trolley is Twitter.

trends.vid: red blue button moral dilemma meme

def.normie: A pop song by British singer-songwriter Sienna Spiro from The Devil Wears Prada 2 soundtrack. Creators are using it on fashion hauls, outfit checks, and aspirational shopping clips — anything with that polished, runway energy.
def.normal: Soundtrack drop turned algorithm gift: 'Material Lover' is still the dominant fashion audio, though the window is narrowing as brand accounts start stitching their lookbooks to it.
def.zoomer: Sienna sang one (1) Devil Wears Prada 2 song and now every fit check has the same backing track. Two weeks max before it's in a Target ad.

trends.name: "Remember who you are" / Freakin' Out

def.normie: Reels trend pairing 'Freakin' Out' by Dexter and the Moonrocks with the phrase 'remember who you are' over travel, training, and personal-growth montages. Cinematic, emotional, very main-character.
def.normal: Adventure-and-transformation audio holding strong into mid-June. Travel and fitness niches own it; glow-up edits are catching up; parody window opens any minute.
def.zoomer: Stand in a windy field. Say 'remember who you are.' Watch the algorithm love you. Then in 4 days, watch the parodies eat it.

trends.origin: Dexter and the Moonrocks 'Freakin' Out' → Reels trend, 2026
trends.vid: remember who you are reels trend

trends.name: Beez in the Trap × What's Up? mashup

def.normie: A mashup blending Nicki Minaj's 'Beez in the Trap' with 4 Non Blondes' 'What's Up?'. The combination of rap menace and emotional rock vocals makes it equal parts funny and dramatic.
def.normal: Still traveling cross-niche but losing momentum. The first bad brand activation (Wendy's, allegedly) accelerated the slide.
def.zoomer: It cooked. Now it's getting cooked. One more week before it becomes a 'remember when' reference.

trends.vid: beez in the trap whats up mashup

trends.name: "no no no NO NO NO"

def.normie: A short audio clip where someone says 'no no no' quietly and then yells 'NO NO NO' as panic sets in.
def.normal: The "I Green Screen Things" sound where escalating "no"s match an escalating crash-out. Now load-bearing on every panic-edit.
def.zoomer: Pure pre-crashout audio. Brilliance is the build: quiet refusal, public denial, the howl. Still undefeated, but the saturation alarm is going off.

trends.origin: I Green Screen Things audio, TikTok 2026

trends.name: "Hate That I Made You Love Me" mashup

def.normie: A mashup using Ariana Grande's song 'Petal' that creators jumped on through early June.
def.normal: Mashup audio that rose with the Petal album drop. Fast-growing lip-sync trend, now slipping.
def.zoomer: It already had its main-character week. Now it's a 'when did this stop being everywhere' candidate.

trends.vid: hate that i made you love me trend

trends.name: KitKat Heist memes

def.normie: Memes about the real-life European theft of around 400,000 KitKat bars.
def.normal: Memes about the real ~400,000 KitKat theft in Europe. Peaked late May; now in reference-meme zone.
def.zoomer: The cinematic universe is canon. KitKat heist is now a vibe descriptor, not a joke.

trends.origin: Real European logistics theft → meme, mid-2026

trends.name: Nihilistic Penguin

def.normie: Old footage of a penguin walking alone toward the mountains instead of the sea.
def.normal: Werner Herzog's 2007 documentary penguin became 2026's mascot for opting out. Still on every LinkedIn motivational post.
def.zoomer: Herzog filmed him in 2007, we caught up emotionally in 2026. The penguin will outlive every other trend on this board.

trends.origin: 'Encounters at the End of the World' (2007) → meme, 2026

def.normie: A format where dramatic saxophone music starts quietly and gets louder as a situation gets worse.
def.normal: The escalation-saxophone format: music swells as the on-screen situation collapses. Still everywhere, slightly past peak.
def.zoomer: The sax already won. We're now in the 'corporate brand uses it' phase.

trends.name: I am my perimeter

def.normie: A streamer's deadpan 'I am my perimeter and my perimeter is me' became a catchphrase.
def.normal: Snapiyya's Rainbow Six Siege line as all-purpose 'locked in' declaration. Now showing up in corporate Slack.
def.zoomer: Bro got rushed and achieved enlightenment on stream. The phrase has moved from gaming to general 'locked in' usage.

trends.origin: Snapiyya / Rainbow Six Siege stream clip, 2026

def.normie: 'AI slop' is the term for cheap, mass-produced AI content clogging up feeds.
def.normal: Backlash against low-effort AI content is dominating commentary feeds. Homer Donut song threw gas on this.
def.zoomer: The Slop Wars: phase three. We are now slop-pilled and at war with ourselves about whether the slop is funny.

def.normie: X rolled out a 'Certified Banger' badge for viral posts and the internet immediately fought about it.
def.normal: X's new viral-post badge launched first week of June and became the discourse. Now cooling — only the cope memes remain.
def.zoomer: The badge was the meme; now the badge is just a feature again. The cope outlasted the discourse, as it should.

def.normie: A movement calling for a return to older, simpler human-made internet humour.
def.normal: The push for nostalgic, authentic memes as reaction to AI slop and brain-rot fatigue. Slightly past peak but still load-bearing.
def.zoomer: The Reset is a meme about wanting better memes, which is correctly very us. Also the Homer Donut song is testing everyone's principles.

def.normie: An insult for robots, AI, or people who rely on AI too much — borrowed from Star Wars.
def.normal: Star Wars' battle-droid slur, repurposed as the internet's everyday anti-AI insult. Now squarely mainstream.
def.zoomer: Said unironically by your dad zone, officially crossed. Still works, but you're tipping yourself off as Online.

def.normie: To completely lose your cool — a meltdown, usually public.
def.normal: Losing emotional control spectacularly; 'a crashout' is the episode or the person. Permanent vocabulary now.
def.zoomer: Officially load-bearing slang. It's in your principal's vocabulary.

def.normie: A way to call someone empty or absent — like the '404 page not found' error.
def.normal: The '-coded' suffix found its tech-meme breakout. Mainstream-adjacent now.
def.zoomer: Radar called it, the timeline confirmed it. Approaching cliché but still usable for one more cycle.

def.normie: Fan theory claiming the Stranger Things finale was an illusion.
def.normal: The viral theory that the Stranger Things finale concealed a hidden episode. Slow burn.
def.zoomer: Denial fandom %, sustained. Speedrun complete; now it's tradition.

def.normie: A mash-up of 'chopped' (unattractive) and 'doppelganger': an unflattering lookalike.
def.normal: 'Chopped' + 'doppelganger' = blunt comparison format. Word will outlive the format that birthed it.
def.zoomer: Word survives, format dead. Use it in conversation, not in a video, if you want to look current.

def.normie: The joke that consuming too much silly internet content rots your brain — Oxford's 2024 word of the year.
def.normal: Universally understood, no longer cutting edge.
def.zoomer: Saying you have brain rot is no longer a flex — it's a census statistic.

battles.q: Which new format is winning this week?
Which AI artifact deserves to survive?

hot: Reels' wholesome-flex format of the week — sincerity is the punchline
AI slop song that the timeline can't decide whether to defeat or embrace
Every niche now runs its own version; the debates outlive the prompts
died: deported by the Great Meme Reset
the irony collapsed in on itself
faded before the algorithm could find it
incoming: Format pitting a high-effort edit against a deadpan static shot, letting the comments vote which wins
tip: Cleanest new format of the week — works on Reels and TikTok, doesn't need a sound license, plays in any niche from fitness to baking. Window: 10-12 days before the parodies own it.
weird: AI slop the Reset crowd actually likes, because the punchline IS the slop. The discourse is more interesting than the song.

radar.name: "Algorithm Prefers" format
radar.sig: This A/B-style format is about to explode
radar.det: Creators pit a high-effort edit against a deadpan static shot and let the comments vote which wins. Format travels across every niche.

radar.name: Game Time / FIFA 2026 audio
radar.det: Future × Tyla 'Game Time' just dropped as part of the FIFA World Cup 2026 soundtrack. Sports and travel niches are early; brand accounts about to flood it.

radar.name: Domestic ASMR sounds
radar.det: Cleaning, packing, morning routines leaning into peel/spray/wipe/tap audio. Making mundane action feel cinematic without a hit song.

radar.name: Clanker court
radar.sig: AI-on-trial skits are now a genre
radar.det: Mock-trial videos putting AI tools and bot accounts on trial are building. Homer Donut song accelerating this.

radar.name: "-coded" suffix wave
radar.det: After 404 coded broke through, error-coded, beta-coded, captcha-coded are incubating.

radar.name: Heatwave content
radar.sig: Weather is about to drive a content wave
radar.det: European heatwave coverage is starting to spawn the familiar summer-discourse cycle.

radar.name: Petal album residue
radar.sig: This sound family is still mutating
radar.det: Beyond 'Hate That I Made You Love Me', more Petal cuts are getting CapCut-templated.

radar.name: Analog posting
radar.sig: The Reset's aesthetic wing is gaining ground
radar.det: Film photos, unedited clips, deliberately low-tech content as anti-slop signalling.
